title: Continuous production of particles
abstract: The present invention discloses a method to continuously manufacture micro- and/or nanoparticles of single component particles or multi-component particles such as particulate amorphous solid dispersions or particulate co-crystals. The continuous method comprises the steps of 1. preparing a first solution comprising at least one component and at least one solvent and a second solution comprising at least one anti-solvent of the at least one component comprised in the first solution, 2. mixing said first solution and said second solution by means of microfluidization to produce a suspension by precipitation or co-precipitation, 3. feeding said suspension to a filtration system to obtain a concentrate stream, 4. feeding said concentrate stream to a spray dryer, 5. atomizing said concentrate stream using at least one atomization nozzle, 6. drying said atomized concentrate stream to obtain particles, and 7. collecting said particles. Single component particles or multi-component particles, particulate amorphous solid dispersions, particulate co-crystals and pharmaceutical compositions are also disclosed.
claims: Claims 1 . A method to continuously manufacture single component particles or multi-component particles comprising the steps of: preparing a first solution comprising at least one component and at least one solvent, and a second solution comprising at least one anti-solvent of the at least one component comprised in the first solution; mixing said first solution and said second solution by means of microfluidization to produce a suspension by precipitation or co-precipitation; feeding said suspension to a filtration system to obtain a concentrate stream; feeding said concentrate stream to a spray dryer; atomizing said concentrate stream using at least one atomization nozzle; drying said atomized concentrate stream to obtain particles; and collecting said particles. 2. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the first solution comprises at least one active pharmaceutical ingredient (API). 3. The method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the first solution comprises zero, one or more than one excipient. 4. The method according to claim 1 , 2 or 3, wherein the second solution comprises at least one API. 5. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the second solution comprises zero, one or more than one excipient. 6. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microfluidization is effected using at least one microreactor, and wherein the at least one microreactor comprises one or more channels each having a diameter in the range of about 10 microns to about 400 microns, optionally in the range of about 50 microns to about 200 microns. 7. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microfluidization is effected using more than one microreactor, and wherein the microreactors are arranged in series or in parallel. 8. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microfluidization is effected using at least one microreactor, and wherein the microreactor is a continuous flow reactor. 9. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the first solution is combined with the second solution at a pressure sufficient to cause interaction of the at least one component, at least one solvent, at least one anti-solvent and any additional reagents present in the solutions; and delivered to one or more channels in a microreactor such that the at least one component, at least one solvent, at least one anti-solvent and any additional reagents present in the solutions react to form a suspension of particles by precipitation or co-precipitation. 10. The method according to claim 10, wherein the pressure is in the range of from about 1 bar to about 3500 bar, optionally from about 20 to about 3500 bar, from about 100 to about 3000 bar, or from about 300 bar to about 2500 bar. 1 1 . The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microfluidization is effected using at least one microreactor, and wherein the method further comprises cooling or quenching the suspension after the solutions have mixed within the at least one microreactor. 12. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the microfluidization is effected using at least one microreactor, and wherein a pump is used to transport the suspension from the at least one microreactor to the filtration system. 13. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the filtration system comprises at least one tangential flow filtration system or at least one cross-flow membrane system. 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein the filtration system comprises at least one cross- flow membrane system, and wherein the pore size of the membrane in the at least one cross- flow membrane system ranges from about 1 nm to about 100 microns, optionally from about 10 nm to about 1 micron. 15. The method according to claim 13 or 14, wherein the filtration system comprises at least one cross-flow membrane system, and wherein the at least one cross-flow membrane system comprises a cross-flow flat sheet membrane, a cross-flow tubular membrane, a cross-flow spiral membrane, a cross-flow hollow fiber membrane and/or a cross-flow cassette membrane, optionally the at least one cross-flow membrane is a cross-flow cassette membrane. 16. The method according to claim 13, 14 or 15, wherein the filtration system comprises at least one cross-flow membrane system, and wherein the at least one cross-flow membrane system comprises an inorganic microsieve type cross-flow membrane or a polymeric track-etched type cross-flow membrane. 17. The method according to any one of claims 13 to 16, wherein the tangential flow filtration systems or cross-flow membrane systems are arranged in series or in parallel. 18. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein the at least one atomization nozzle comprises a rotary type nozzle, a pressure nozzle, a fluid nozzle, or an ultrasonic nozzle. 19. The method according to any preceding claim, wherein drying is promoted by a gas stream. 20. The method according to claim 19, wherein the gas stream is co-current or counter-current with respect to the atomized concentrate stream direction. 21 . The method according to claim 19 or 20, wherein the gas stream comprises nitrogen, air, carbon dioxide or a combination thereof. 22. Single component particles or multi-component particles obtainable according to any one of the preceding claims. 23. Single component particles or multi-component particles according to claim 22, wherein the particles comprise from about 5 to about 100% (w/w), optionally from about 5 to about 95% (w/w) of at least one API and from about 95 to about 5% (w/w) of one or more than one excipient. 24. Single component particles or multi-component particles according to claim 22 or 23, wherein the product comprises 0% of a surfactant or comprises greater than 0% surfactant. 25. Single component particles or multi-component particles according to claim 22, 23 or 24, wherein the particles have a particle size ranging from nano-range to micro-range. 26. Single component particles or multi-component particles according to claim 25, wherein the particle size is in the range of from about 50 nm to about 10 Î¼Î·Î¹, optionally in the range of from about 50 nm to about 2 Î¼Î·Î¹, in the range of from about 50 nm to about 1 Î¼Î·Î¹, or in the range of 50 nm to about 800 nm. 27. Single component particles or multi-component particles according to any one of claims 22 to 26, wherein the particles have a bulk density in the range of from about 0.1 g/ml to about 1 .0 g/ml. 28. Single component particles or multi-component particles according to any one of claims 22 to 27, for use in increasing the bioavailability of an API. 29. Single component particles or multi-component particles according to any one of claims 22 to 28, wherein the particles comprise fluticasone propionate. 30. A pharmaceutical composition comprising single component particles or multi-component particles according to any one of claims 22 to 29. 31 . A pharmaceutical composition according to claim 30, for use as a medicament. 32. A particulate amorphous solid dispersion obtainable according to any of claims 1 to 21 . 33. A particulate amorphous solid dispersion according to claim 32, wherein the amorphous solid dispersion comprises from about 5 to about 95% (w/w) of at least one API and from about 95 to about 5% (w/w) of one or more than one excipient. 34. The particulate amorphous solid dispersion according to claim 32 or 33, wherein the particles have a particle size ranging from nano-range to micro-range. 35. The particulate amorphous solid dispersion according to claim 34, wherein the particle size is in the range of from about 50 nm to about 1 Î¼Î·Î¹, or in the range of 50 nm to about 800 nm. 36. The particulate amorphous solid dispersion according to any one of claims 32 to 35, wherein the particles have a bulk density in the range of from about 0.1 g/ml to about 1 .0 g/ml. 37. The particulate amorphous solid dispersion according to any one of claims 32 to 36, for use in increasing the bioavailability of an API. 38. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a particulate amorphous solid dispersion according to any one of claims 32 to 37. 39. A pharmaceutical composition according to claim 38, for use as a medicament. 40. A particulate co-crystal obtainable according to any one of claims 1 to 21 . 41 . A particulate co-crystal according to claim 40, wherein the particulate co-crystal comprises from about 5 to about 95% (w/w) of at least one API and from about 95 to about 5% (w/w) of one or more than one excipient. 42. The particulate co-crystal according to claim 40 or 41 , wherein the particles have a particle size ranging from nano-range to micro-range. 43. The particulate co-crystal according to claim 42, wherein the particle size is in the range of from about 50 nm to about 10Î¼Î·Î¹, or in the range of from about 50 nm to about 2 Î¼Î·Î¹. 44. The particulate co-crystal according to any one of claims 40 to 43, wherein the particles have a bulk density in the range of from about 0.1 g/ml to about 1 .0 g/ml. 45. The particulate co-crystal according to any one of claims 40 to 44, wherein the co-crystal purity is at least 50% (w/w), optionally at least 75% (w/w), or at least 90% (w/w). 46. The particulate co-crystal according to any one of claims 40 to 45, for use in increasing the aqueous solubility, bioavailability, hygroscopicity, stability and/or taste of an API. 47. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a particulate co-crystal according to any one of claims 40 to 46. 48. A pharmaceutical composition according to claim 47, for use as a medicament. 49. A method as described herein with reference to the Examples and Figures. 50. Single component particles or multi-component particles as described herein with reference to the Examples and Figures. 51 . A particulate amorphous solid dispersion as described herein with reference to the Examples and Figures. 52. A particulate co-crystal as described herein with reference to the Examples and Figures. 53. A pharmaceutical composition as described herein with reference to the Examples.
